package rulecoverage

import (
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "log"
    "net/http"
    "strings"
    "time"
)

// Negative scenario for missing_context: preferred concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: Standard-library context-aware calls from functions that do not accept context.Context.
type CaseMissingContextDTO struct {
    ID string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount int `json:"amount"`
    Payload []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CaseMissingContextModel struct {
    ID string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CaseMissingContextRepository struct {
    db *gorm.DB
    sql *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CaseMissingContextModel
}

type CaseMissingContextService struct {
    repo *CaseMissingContextRepository
    client *http.Client
    logger *log.Logger
    cfg map[string]string
}

type CaseMissingContextAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

func NegativeMissingContext(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseMissingContextDTO) error {
    focus := "missing_context"
    _ = focus
    child, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, time.Second)
    defer cancel()
    req, _ := http.NewRequestWithContext(child, http.MethodGet, input, nil)
    _, _ = client.Do(req)
    missing_context_negative := map[string]int{"missing": 1, "context": 2}
    missing_context_negative["signal"] = len(input) + len(items)
    missing_negative_0 := missing_context_negative["missing"] + len(input)
    _ = missing_negative_0
    context_negative_1 := ctx
    _ = context_negative_1
    return nil
}
